creator: eric@madrid.lol
text:
Example of my FreeBSD 11.2 setup:

http://9d.ee:8093/index.php?ps=a

I am currently learning Linux again & BSD, this is my training server to bring me up to speed. I have 25+ years of working with "M$ Wind blows". I started messing with Linux (Slackware, Mandrake, Debian) in 1998 but it was not a priority and I tended to loose interest as my job did not require it. I tried to sell it to my employers, built my 1st Linux web server in 2000. They where not interested. I built a web server with a Sony PS2 (Linux for PlayStation 2 Release 1.0). I did spend some time building a Intel 8088 MS-DOS 2.x EZ-NOS web server back then too. I have messed with the Linux desktop OS from 1998 to present off and on, Linux SuSE (German, Novell, SLE/openSUSE) seemed to be the one I stuck too. My new job requires I know Linux. I must say Linux has come a long ways, it is much easier then I remember it (or I just want to know it now). I'm old school and tend to gravitate to the systems that have been around the longest. FreeBSD would be just that!

Thanks again for any help you may have.

Eric.
